language_stack.primary_evidence: 2118 .py files; pyproject.toml thegent v0.x Python CLI (typer, fastmcp, pydantic, opentelemetry); 24+ thegent-* Rust crates expose Python bindings via thegent.rust_wrappers:clode/roid/droid/dex/anen/fanta/antigma
language_stack.secondary_evidence: 192 .rs files under crates/ (24-crate workspace: thegent-parser, thegent-discovery, thegent-git, thegent-cache, thegent-crypto, thegent-fs, thegent-hooks, thegent-tui, thegent-memory, thegent-router, thegent-metrics, thegent-policy, thegent-shm, thegent-zmx, thegent-plugin-host, etc.); 49 .ts/.tsx + VitePress site under docs/; extensive zsh/bash dotfiles in dotfiles/ and shell/
language_stack.size_signal: 1.3GB on disk; 1993 commits; apps/byteport + apps/landing + services/argisroute + services/colab + sub-projects/thegent-sharecli co-located

README.detail: README.md 13334b with work-state header, mermaid architecture diagram, CLI command reference, dotfile/template coverage

CI.detail: .github/workflows/ has 20+ workflows: ci.yml, codeql.yml, scorecard.yml, cargo-deny.yml, quality-gate.yml, policy-gate.yml, pr-governance-gate.yml, license-compliance.yml, fuzzing.yml, coverage.yml, sast*.yml, security-guard.yml, security-deep-scan.yml, iac-scan.yml, release.yml, pages-deploy.yml, ai-testing.yml

hygiene_notes: All 10 hygiene artifacts present and substantive — thegent is the best-in-fleet on hygiene signals.
README has explicit `Work state: ACTIVE · Progress: 60%` header aligned with the fleet-wide work-state convention.
CI is over-provisioned: 20+ workflows plus a policy-gate and pr-governance-gate — a candidate for rationalization if CI minutes become a cost concern.

dup_exposure.rationale: thegent is the polyglot development hub of the Phenotype fleet and ships its own event bus, request-id, registry, metrics, and tooling layers. Sibling phenotype-* / pheno* libraries cover most of these surfaces and represent convergence opportunities.

consumable.replaces: thegent-router + thegent-runtime inline pub-sub + thegent-events / thegent-maif event fan-out
manual cargo-deny + ad-hoc dependabot config in crates/Cargo.toml
scattered `run_id` / `request_id` string threading across thegent_*.py modules
thegent-discovery + thegent-runtime service registry (subset of surface)
any icon set duplicated in apps/landing or docs/ that the team re-uses
scripts/* + Justfile + Taskfile.yml + pre-commit-config.yaml overlap (Justfile is a recent add per DAG stage 4 commit)
crates/thegent-utils + duplicated helper modules in thegent-tui / thegent-fs / thegent-router
thegent agent-role specs scattered under src/agent_roles and thegent_agents.py
apps/byteport ad-hoc audit logging in backend/ + audit_trail hooks in governance/
scripts/vitepress-agent-workflow.py + scripts/generate-* + docs/ — the entire docs tooling stack

mirror_worktree_collapse_flag.issue: thegent ships 4 sibling worktree directories; two of them are EXACT mirrors (same SHA 437a34de6d0aa651555d82ca44006b34af6c07f1 and same branch chore/profile-tighten-2026-06-08): thegent-security-fixes-wtrees/profile-tighten-2026-06-08 and thegent-wtrees/profile-tighten-2026-06-08. thegent-security-fixes (chore/security-2026-06-08) is a third worktree on the same repo. Only the first one (thegent/) is the canonical checkout; the other three are worktree mirrors that should be collapsed via `git worktree remove` once their branches are merged or rebased onto main.
mirror_worktree_collapse_flag.evidence: `git worktree list` shows 4 entries; 2 share both SHA and branch; 1 extra sits on a different branch on the same SHA family (a2af7ccad).
mirror_worktree_collapse_flag.remediation: Run `git worktree remove` on the two exact-mirror worktrees after merging chore/profile-tighten-2026-06-08 into main; remove the third (thegent-security-fixes) once chore/security-2026-06-08 lands.

sota_gaps.rationale: Compared to state-of-the-art CLI agent harnesses in 2026 (claude-code, codex exec, gemini-cli, aider, goose, kilo) and to the fleet-wide phenotype-* SOTA baseline, the following capabilities are missing or stubbed in thegent.

gaps.feature: MLX / OMLX local model provider first-class support
gaps.evidence: phenotype-omlx exists in the fleet; thegent's pyproject has litellm==1.87.1 as a dev dep but no OMLX/MLX provider wired into the provider router (thegent-router / model_providers)

gaps.feature: Plugin / extension marketplace with declarative manifests and signed bundles
gaps.evidence: thegent-plugin-host crate exists but no manifest schema or signature verification; extensions/ + plugins/ dirs scattered without a unifying protocol

gaps.feature: Multi-modal input (image, PDF, audio attachments) end-to-end
gaps.evidence: FastMCP resources and pydantic schemas in place, but no renderer/attachment pipeline in the CLI or docs/ frontend

gaps.feature: OTEL OTLP exporter wired to phenotype-bus (telemetry beyond local handlers)
gaps.evidence: opentelemetry-api/sdk in pyproject; thegent-metrics crate present; no OTLP exporter wired to a shared bus
thegent-subprocess crate present; no checked-in SBPL/seatbelt profile or landlock rule set; no bubblewrap wrapper

gaps.feature: Structured-output / JSON-schema strict tool calling with auto-retry on schema mismatch
gaps.evidence: pydantic available; no centralized schema-validation retry loop surfaced in the provider router

gaps.feature: Headless non-interactive exec mode (`thegent exec --quiet --json`) for CI parity
gaps.evidence: thegent.cli.apps.main:app entrypoint; no documented --json / non-interactive contract for automation consumers

gaps.feature: Nix `apps.*` + `packages.*` outputs and cross-platform single-binary release
gaps.evidence: no flake.nix declared at root; release.yml exists but no cargo-dist / nix app outputs surfaced
thegent-tui crate present; no audio capture or whisper-backed transcript pipeline

gaps.feature: Workstream autosync bidirectional (GitHub Projects + Linear) hardened for org-scale
gaps.evidence: README mentions 'Workstream Autosync' under core; no schema for conflict resolution surfaced; phenotype-org-audits in fleet could converge here

convergence_cluster.label: multi-stack
convergence_cluster.rationale: Single repo spans 3+ language stacks (Python CLI/Rust crates/TypeScript VitePress) plus extensive shell dotfiles and per-app satellites (apps/byteport, apps/landing, services/argisroute, services/colab, sub-projects/thegent-sharecli). It is the polyglot hub of the fleet, not a single converged product surface — too heterogeneous for 'web-stack' and not a deployed production system for 'live'. Best fit: multi-stack.

action_items.task: Collapse the 4 thegent worktrees — `git worktree remove` the two exact mirrors at thegent-wtrees/profile-tighten-2026-06-08 and thegent-security-fixes-wtrees/profile-tighten-2026-06-08 (both at SHA 437a34de6 on chore/profile-tighten-2026-06-08), then merge that branch into main and remove the third (thegent-security-fixes) once chore/security-2026-06-08 lands
Wire MLX / OMLX local model provider into the provider router and the Rust thegent-router crate so thegent exposes phenotype-omlx end-to-end
Adopt phenotype-bus + phenotype-request-id across thegent-router, thegent-runtime, thegent-tui, and the Python thegent_*.py modules to drop local re-implementations
Add SBPL/seatbelt (macOS) and landlock (Linux) sandbox profiles for thegent-subprocess + thegent-cli, and gate tool execution on them
Stabilize `thegent exec --quiet --json` non-interactive output contract and document it for fleet automation consumers (KWatch, heliosBench, phenoDevOps)
